error.signature: Azure AD token works for wrong tenant or returns 'AADSTS50020: User account does not exist in tenant'

verdict.summary: MSAL token cache uses authority URL as part of the cache key. If you create multiple ConfidentialClientApplication instances with different tenants but share the same cache, tokens leak across tenants.

dead_ends.action: Use a single shared MSAL token cache for all tenants
dead_ends.why_fails: Tokens from tenant A may be served for tenant B requests. The cache key includes authority but bugs in cache partitioning cause cross-tenant leaks.

dead_ends.action: Create a new ConfidentialClientApplication per request
dead_ends.why_fails: Bypasses caching entirely. Every request does a full token acquisition, adding 200-500ms latency.

workarounds.action: Use separate token caches per tenant (partition by tenant_id)
Use MSAL's built-in cache partitioning with partition_key
